message.content: Draft a 200-word intro for a blog post titled "Driving Claude Code from a phone".

content.text:
Draft below — punchy, sets up the rest of the piece, doesn't oversell.

---

**Driving Claude Code from a phone**

Claude Code is a TUI. TUIs do not love phones. Soft keyboards have no Esc, no Ctrl, no arrows; the URL bar steals viewport height; mobile Safari throws away your scroll position every time the keyboard opens. After three weeks of trying to make a generic terminal-on-the-web project work on Android Chrome, I gave up and wrote one designed for it.

ttyview is a tmux viewer that ships a structured cell grid + cell-diff event stream instead of xterm.js + a PTY pipe. The whole UI is plugins — including the cell renderer. That sounds like overkill until you want a 🎤 button that does live dictation into a CC prompt, a pinned-tabs row, a paste-image-into-the-conversation flow, a header widget showing your token burn rate. None of those belong in core. All of them are 50–150 lines of JS.

This post walks through the four design decisions that made it usable: the cell grid, the plugin contract, the mobile-first input pipeline, and the read-only daemon mode for spectator demos.

message.content: Cut the last sentence — too meta. End on the 'none of those belong in core' beat.

content.text:
Agreed — the meta sentence was telegraphing the structure instead of letting the reader feel the hook. New ending:

> … None of those belong in core. All of them are 50–150 lines of JS.

Stops cleanly. The next section heading will do the work of "here's what we'll cover."
